source_idiom: one could hear a pin drop
source_meaning: Extremely quiet, for example, as during a lull in a normally bustling place or scene, or at a sudden dramatic or tense moment.
target_meaning: the tranquil and desolate scenery in Buddhist temples

source_meaning: Two people united by marriage.
target_meaning: men whose wives are biological sisters in the same family

target_idiom: cẩn tắc vô ưu, vô áy náy
source_meaning: To be cautious.
target_meaning: better safe than sorry

source_meaning: To pace back and forth restlessly, because of worry, excitement, distress, etc.
target_meaning: to be restless; to feel uneasy; to be on tenterhooks; to fidget

source_meaning: be jealous
target_meaning: to be bursting with jealousy; to be jealous; to hate on others because of jealousy

source_meaning: An enticing but dangerous appeal, especially a misleading one.
target_meaning: charming beauty who makes men fall and leads to disasters (such as a loss of wealth or reputation, or a toppled nation); dangerous woman; femme fatale

source_meaning: A disaster; a chaotic person, thing, or situation.
target_meaning: a situation where many people become homeless, especially after a disaster

source_meaning: discard
target_meaning: synonym of đem con bỏ chợ

source_meaning: To lose a large amount of money in an investment.
target_meaning: to lose everything, including initial investment

target_idiom: miếng trầu là đầu câu chuyện
source_meaning: A tart dialogue with quick replies.
target_meaning: paan is a conversation starter

source_meaning: To spend money foolishly or indiscriminately; to waste money without regard of the consequences.
target_meaning: to spend one's money without generating any income; to fritter away a great fortune

target_idiom: một giọt máu đào hơn ao nước lã
source_meaning: Family relations and loyalties are stronger than relationships with people who are not family members.
target_meaning: blood is thicker than water

source_idiom: the chickens come home to roost
target_idiom: ngậm máu phun người, trước dơ miệng mình
source_meaning: A person's past wrongdoings will return to negatively affect them.
target_meaning: Curses are like young chickens, they always come home to roost.

source_meaning: To smile a very beaming smile
target_meaning: a beautiful woman's smile is extremely precious

source_meaning: To cry insincerely or in an exaggerated manner.
target_meaning: crocodile tears

source_meaning: To make promises one cannot keep, or to make boasts that are not true.
target_meaning: empty promise; vain promise; false promise

source_idiom: take the law into one's own hands
source_meaning: To punish someone according to one's own idea of justice, without consideration for the role of law enforcement authorities.
target_meaning: to enforce the (moral) Way on behalf of Heaven, to take the law into one's own hands

source_meaning: Impotence caused by heavy drinking.
target_meaning: to impotent, unable to achieve or sustain an erection, having impotentia coeundi

source_meaning: up to here
target_meaning: all the way

source_meaning: one and all
target_meaning: all men are brothers

source_meaning: A relatively minor annoyance or inconvenience, jokingly implied to be the "last straw" that will motivate someone to commit suicide.
target_meaning: the straw that broke the camel's back

source_meaning: Used to express gratitude at unnecessary generosity, especially when receiving a gift.
target_meaning: to be grateful to one's benefactor

source_idiom: the pen is mightier than the sword
source_meaning: More influence and power can be usurped by writing than by fighting.
target_meaning: good at both the arts of fighting/warring and writing

source_meaning: A green vegetable dye from China
target_meaning: synonym of ba que xỏ lá

source_idiom: couldn't carry a note in a bucket
source_meaning: Not musically talented, unable to sing.
target_meaning: singers and (more generally) musicians are immoral
